title: Carte Blanche digs into Knysna woes
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"KNYSNA NEWS - Administration, or no administration by the Western Cape provincial government - that is the question on everyone’s lips after the Carte Blanche episode on Sunday 10 August which again highlighted Knysa’s service delivery woes.","html":"KNYSNA NEWS - Administration, or no administration by the Western Cape provincial government - that is the question on everyone’s lips after the Carte Blanche episode on Sunday 10 August which again highlighted Knysa’s service delivery woes."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The main thrust of Western Cape local government MEC Anton Bredell was that provincial authorities have worked with Knysna for several years, spending money (R50m) and time with little results.","html":"The main thrust of Western Cape local government MEC Anton Bredell was that provincial authorities have worked with Knysna for several years, spending money (R50m) and time with little results."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Knysna Mayor Thando Matika said: “Give us time. We are dealing with it”, stating the ruling coalition had brought back financial stability to the town."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Knysna Infrastructure Group is opposed to administration, as is the Knysna Independent Movement (KIM).","html":"The Knysna Infrastructure Group is opposed to administration, as is the Knysna Independent Movement (KIM)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Read more in this week's edition of the Knysna-Plett Herald.","html":"Read more in this week's edition of the Knysna-Plett Herald."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Carte Blanche digs into Knysna woes
